item.title: About
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Some people write to be understood. I write because I don’t understand myself yet. — Teddy

item.title: Policy
item.author: Teddy
item.description: A demo policy for a no cookies, privacy friendly website
item.content:
Demo Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy describes our policies and procedures on the collection, use and disclosure of your information when you use the service and tells you about your privacy rights and how the law protects You.

We use your personal data to provide and improve the service. By using the service, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this Privacy Policy. This Privacy Policy has been created with the help of the Privacy Policy Generator.

Interpretation and Definitions

Interpretation

The words of which the initial letter is capitalized have meanings defined under the following conditions. The following definitions shall have the same meaning regardless of whether they appear in singular or in plural.

Definitions

For the purposes of this Privacy Policy:

  • Account means a unique account created for You to access our Service or parts of our Service.
  • Affiliate means an entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party, where “control” means ownership of 50% or more of the shares, equity interest or other securities entitled to vote for election of directors or other managing authority.
  • Company (referred to as either “the Company”, “We”, “Us” or “Our” in this Agreement) refers to My Site.
  • Cookies are small files that are placed on Your computer, mobile device or any other device by a website, containing the details of Your browsing history on that website among its many uses.
  • Country refers to: Berlin, Germany
  • Device means any device that can access the Service such as a computer, a cellphone or a digital tablet.
  • Personal Data is any information that relates to an identified or identifiable individual.
  • Service refers to the Website.
  • Service Provider means any natural or legal person who processes the data on behalf of the Company. It refers to third-party companies or individuals employed by the Company to facilitate the Service, to provide the Service on behalf of the Company, to perform services related to the Service or to assist the Company in analyzing how the Service is used.
  • Usage Data refers to data collected automatically, either generated by the use of the Service or from the Service infrastructure itself (for example, the duration of a page visit).
  • Website refers to My Site, accessible from https://www.example.com
  • You means the individual accessing or using the Service, or the company, or other legal entity on behalf of which such individual is accessing or using the Service, as applicable.

Types of Data Collected

Personal Data

While using Our Service, We may ask You to provide Us with certain personally identifiable information that can be used to contact or identify You.

Usage Data

Usage Data is collected automatically when using the Service.

Usage Data may include information such as Your Device’s Internet Protocol address (e.g. IP address), browser type, browser version, the pages of our Service that You visit, the time and date of Your visit, the time spent on those pages, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data.

When You access the Service by or through a mobile device, We may collect certain information automatically, including, but not limited to, the type of mobile device You use, Your mobile device unique ID, the IP address of Your mobile device, Your mobile operating system, the type of mobile Internet browser You use, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data.

We may also collect information that Your browser sends whenever You visit our Service or when You access the Service by or through a mobile device.

Tracking Technologies and Cookies

We use Cookies and similar tracking technologies to track the activity on Our Service and store certain information. Tracking technologies used are beacons, tags, and scripts to collect and track information and to improve and analyze Our Service. The technologies We use may include:

  • Cookies or Browser Cookies. A cookie is a small file placed on Your Device. You can instruct Your browser to refuse all Cookies or to indicate when a Cookie is being sent. However, if You do not accept Cookies, You may not be able to use some parts of our Service. Unless you have adjusted Your browser setting so that it will refuse Cookies, our Service may use Cookies.
  • Web Beacons. Certain sections of our Service and our emails may contain small electronic files known as web beacons (also referred to as clear gifs, pixel tags, and single-pixel gifs) that permit the Company, for example, to count users who have visited those pages or opened an email and for other related website statistics (for example, recording the popularity of a certain section and verifying system and server integrity).

Cookies can be “Persistent” or “Session” Cookies. Persistent Cookies remain on Your personal computer or mobile device when You go offline, while Session Cookies are deleted as soon as You close Your web browser. You can learn more about cookies on TermsFeed website article.

We use both Session and Persistent Cookies for the purposes set out below:

  • Necessary / Essential Cookies

    Type: Session Cookies

    Administered by: Us

    Purpose: These Cookies are essential to provide You with services available through the Website and to enable You to use some of its features. They help to authenticate users and prevent fraudulent use of user accounts. Without these Cookies, the services that You have asked for cannot be provided, and We only use these Cookies to provide You with those services.

  • Cookies Policy / Notice Acceptance Cookies

    Type: Persistent Cookies

    Administered by: Us

    Purpose: These Cookies identify if users have accepted the use of cookies on the Website.

  • Functionality Cookies

    Type: Persistent Cookies

    Administered by: Us

    Purpose: These Cookies allow us to remember choices You make when You use the Website, such as remembering your login details or language preference. The purpose of these Cookies is to provide You with a more personal experience and to avoid You having to re-enter your preferences every time You use the Website.

For more information about the cookies we use and your choices regarding cookies, please visit our Cookies Policy or the Cookies section of our Privacy Policy.

Use of Your Personal Data

The Company may use Personal Data for the following purposes:

  • To provide and maintain our Service, including to monitor the usage of our Service.

  • To manage Your Account: to manage Your registration as a user of the Service. The Personal Data You provide can give You access to different functionalities of the Service that are available to You as a registered user.

  • For the performance of a contract: the development, compliance and undertaking of the purchase contract for the products, items or services You have purchased or of any other contract with Us through the Service.

  • To contact You: To contact You by email, telephone calls, SMS, or other equivalent forms of electronic communication, such as a mobile application’s push notifications regarding updates or informative communications related to the functionalities, products or contracted services, including the security updates, when necessary or reasonable for their implementation.

  • To provide You with news, special offers and general information about other goods, services and events which we offer that are similar to those that you have already purchased or enquired about unless You have opted not to receive such information.

  • To manage Your requests: To attend and manage Your requests to Us.

  • For business transfers: We may use Your information to evaluate or conduct a merger, divestiture, restructuring, reorganization, dissolution, or other sale or transfer of some or all of Our assets, whether as a going concern or as part of bankruptcy, liquidation, or similar proceeding, in which Personal Data held by Us about our Service users is among the assets transferred.

  • For other purposes: We may use Your information for other purposes, such as data analysis, identifying usage trends, determining the effectiveness of our promotional campaigns and to evaluate and improve our Service, products, services, marketing and your experience.

We may share Your personal information in the following situations:

  • With Service Providers: We may share Your personal information with Service Providers to monitor and analyze the use of our Service, to contact You.
  • For business transfers: We may share or transfer Your personal information in connection with, or during negotiations of, any merger, sale of Company assets, financing, or acquisition of all or a portion of Our business to another company.
  • With Affiliates: We may share Your information with Our affiliates, in which case we will require those affiliates to honor this Privacy Policy. Affiliates include Our parent company and any other subsidiaries, joint venture partners or other companies that We control or that are under common control with Us.
  • With business partners: We may share Your information with Our business partners to offer You certain products, services or promotions.
  • With other users: when You share personal information or otherwise interact in the public areas with other users, such information may be viewed by all users and may be publicly distributed outside.
  • With Your consent: We may disclose Your personal information for any other purpose with Your consent.

Retention of Your Personal Data

The Company will retain Your Personal Data only for as long as is necessary for the purposes set out in this Privacy Policy. We will retain and use Your Personal Data to the extent necessary to comply with our legal obligations (for example, if we are required to retain your data to comply with applicable laws), resolve disputes, and enforce our legal agreements and policies.

The Company will also retain Usage Data for internal analysis purposes. Usage Data is generally retained for a shorter period of time, except when this data is used to strengthen the security or to improve the functionality of Our Service, or We are legally obligated to retain this data for longer time periods.

Transfer of Your Personal Data

Your information, including Personal Data, is processed at the Company’s operating offices and in any other places where the parties involved in the processing are located. It means that this information may be transferred to — and maintained on — computers located outside of Your state, province, country or other governmental jurisdiction where the data protection laws may differ than those from Your jurisdiction.

Your consent to this Privacy Policy followed by Your submission of such information represents Your agreement to that transfer.

The Company will take all steps reasonably necessary to ensure that Your data is treated securely and in accordance with this Privacy Policy and no transfer of Your Personal Data will take place to an organization or a country unless there are adequate controls in place including the security of Your data and other personal information.

Delete Your Personal Data

You have the right to delete or request that We assist in deleting the Personal Data that We have collected about You.

Our Service may give You the ability to delete certain information about You from within the Service.

You may update, amend, or delete Your information at any time by signing in to Your Account, if you have one, and visiting the account settings section that allows you to manage Your personal information. You may also contact Us to request access to, correct, or delete any personal information that You have provided to Us.

Please note, however, that We may need to retain certain information when we have a legal obligation or lawful basis to do so.

Disclosure of Your Personal Data

Business Transactions

If the Company is involved in a merger, acquisition or asset sale, Your Personal Data may be transferred. We will provide notice before Your Personal Data is transferred and becomes subject to a different Privacy Policy.

Law enforcement

Under certain circumstances, the Company may be required to disclose Your Personal Data if required to do so by law or in response to valid requests by public authorities (e.g. a court or a government agency).

Other legal requirements

The Company may disclose Your Personal Data in the good faith belief that such action is necessary to:

  • Comply with a legal obligation
  • Protect and defend the rights or property of the Company
  • Prevent or investigate possible wrongdoing in connection with the Service
  • Protect the personal safety of Users of the Service or the public
  • Protect against legal liability

Security of Your Personal Data

The security of Your Personal Data is important to Us, but remember that no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage is 100% secure. While We strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect Your Personal Data, We cannot guarantee its absolute security.

Children’s Privacy

Our Service does not address anyone under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from anyone under the age of 13. If You are a parent or guardian and You are aware that Your child has provided Us with Personal Data, please contact Us. If We become aware that We have collected Personal Data from anyone under the age of 13 without verification of parental consent, We take steps to remove that information from Our servers.

If We need to rely on consent as a legal basis for processing Your information and Your country requires consent from a parent, We may require Your parent’s consent before We collect and use that information.

Links to Other Websites

Our Service may contain links to other websites that are not operated by Us. If You click on a third party link, You will be directed to that third party’s site. We strongly advise You to review the Privacy Policy of every site You visit.

We have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies or practices of any third party sites or services.

Changes to this Privacy Policy

We may update Our Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify You of any changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page.

We will let You know via email and/or a prominent notice on Our Service, prior to the change becoming effective and update the “Last updated” date at the top of this Privacy Policy.

You are advised to review this Privacy Policy periodically for any changes. Changes to this Privacy Policy are effective when they are posted on this page.

Contact Us

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, You can contact us:

  • By email: contact@example.com

item.title: Love, Time, and Messages
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It finally happened. It was only funny when I was theorycrafting about it, but now that it really happened, I don’t know how to handle this.

Have you ever thought about or even dreamt of time travel? Or something similar? Well, not exactly traveling, but some kind of phenomenon related to time manipulation.

It happened to me—well, in a way. It happened to her as well. The issue is that only one of us knows. Well, technically two, because she counts as two. For fuck’s sake, time travel is complicated.

Let me start simply and keep it short.

I was messaging this girl I met a few days ago. She was funny and interesting—right up my alley, you know. Out of nowhere, I started receiving messages via two different chats in my chat app. At first, I didn’t think much of it because it seemed like a quirky glitch. But after a few days, it hit me—slowly, though.

In one chat, the most recent one, she was far more flirty and familiar. It felt like we’d known each other for years. I brushed it off as her usual funny self, but it was more than that. I got curious, and being the idiot I am, I asked her directly. Our conversation went somewhat like this:

Me : Hey, so I was gonna ask you something.

Her : Yes, what is it now?

Me : Um, this might sound weird, though.

Her : Okay, I guess. It’s not like we’re strangers or anything. And I know you, so nothing can weird me out about you.

Me : See, that’s the thing. That… ugh. Okay, who are you?

Her : What do you mean? Is this one of your pranks? I’m [redacted].

Me : Okay, but why are you being so flirty? We’ve known each other for, like, 10 or 15 days now.

Her : 10 or 15 days? What the actual fuck, man. Is this a joke?

Me : No, not really.

Her : Oh… oh. You’re serious. Oh no, that only means one thing.

Me : What?

Her : No, this can’t be happening.

Me : What can’t be happening?

Her : Okay, you’re a smart guy, right?

Me : Umm… I think so? Maybe?

Her : Listen to me very carefully. There’s been a little issue with our time manipulator. Don’t think too hard about it. It’s from the future and nothing you need to worry about. What I want you to do now is don’t tell the other me anything. Can you promise me that?

Me : Okay.

Her : That’s it? Just okay? No questions like “What’s a time manipulator?” or “When are you from?” Nothing?

Me : I mean, if it’s something related to time, I’ll eventually know about it, right? Less I know is better?

Her : Smart boy. That’s why I married you.

Me : Sorry, what?

Her : Oops. Spoilers. I’ll fix this so you won’t be able to message this chat anymore. It’ll remove itself from your device for… reasons. But hey, it was nice peeking into the past. Save yourself for me, okay?

Me : Wait, married? What?

Although I sent the message, it didn’t deliver, and I couldn’t find the chat afterward. It was the most surreal few minutes of my life up to that point. I almost chalked it up to a prank, but a chat suddenly disappearing like that kind of made it convincing.

So, married. Who would’ve thought?

Come to think of it, I guess I will in the future.

But now, I can’t stop thinking about what she said. Time manipulator? A future me she’s already married to? I’m torn between excitement and dread. If this is real, what does it mean for the choices I make from now on?

I’ve always been curious about the future, but now I’m a little scared to meet it. Still, if she’s right, maybe… just maybe, the future’s looking pretty good after all.

item.title: Into the sunset - A Short story
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It isn’t easy, you know. Eventually, you’ll start to see me as I see myself right now, and the feelings you have for me will fade away like they never existed in the first place.

You see, love as we define it is relative to the opinion we have on someone. As long as you consider me as a proper option to share your time with and tolerate the existence of me beside you till death do us apart, you will continue to love me. If and when you lose that opinion, you’ll start to see the flaws you actively ignored till now.

The habits, traits, look, and all the other things you ignored because your opinion will haunt you over and over again till there’s nothing left of my image you had before.

Knowing this, will you be able to love me? If at some point in our lives, when you start to me as I see myself, would you start to hate me? Or would you be indifferent?

I spoke my train of thought without giving any consideration.

You know, you’re funny. She said.

Why? I had to ask.

I don’t want to see you in someone else’s eyes, not even yours. I see you as it is and I love you for who you are. The problems of the future are yet to happen. Can we just be true to ourselves and just enjoy our time together. We’ll face life as it comes. Don’t worry, I’ll be here to hold your hand if things get rough.

She stopped.

You’re weird. I told her as we both walked into sunset, talking about everything but love.

Photo by Sebastian Voortman on Pexels.com

Hi. I texted her.

Hey, how are you? It’s been a while, isn’t it? She replied.

I’m okay, I guess. Although I typed, I was hesitant to press that send key. Was I okay? Is it something that I can say to someone? I was never okay. But then again, anybody ever is? All of us have our battles we’re barely getting through. Some are winning it, some are losing it.

I pressed the send button.

I wanted to tell her about my demons and everything I’m going through, but she’s not someone I want to shift my burdens into. If I’m being honest, no one is.

We carry this immense burden hoping to put it down someday and be truly happy about life, but until then we’re destined to carry it. There are no shortcuts to happiness. I’ve experienced it, and it comes at a great cost.

You don’t get to be happy unless you’re ready for what follows. It may come sooner or later, but its arrival is inevitable. The sorrow and sadness that paired together with the happiness we acquired will be heavy for you to go through.

You can only hope that you won’t have to go through it in your youth. You simply won’t recover from it. Call it depression or whatever other fancy names you can come up with, but in the end, it’s just an emotional burden of your own.

The conversation went on as usual with her. Both of us not knowing about our demons, battling through it, yet trying to be a positive presence in each other’s lives because that’s what good people do or that’s what we think they do.

And I’m grateful for her. For not asking about them. Some might say that it’s wrong not to but to me, not sharing that makes it easier to keep us as to how it is now.

Do you want to meet up later? She asked.

Sure, why not. Same place? I need some sunset in my life right now. I replied.

Photo by Bruno Scramgnon on Pexels.com

Does love ever fade? I muttered.

Huh!? She got confused with the sudden question.

I mean, If we truly love someone, does it ever fade? The feelings you have of them. Even if it didn’t work out in the end, feelings would remain. And that residual feelings will hurt you over and over till you get tired of them. When I look at her, that’s pretty much the only thing I can think of. If we ever become strangers again, would I be able to go through with it?

Hey, Hello? Earth to Moon, can you hear me? Hi? She shook me out of my trance.

Are you okay? She asked.

Yeah, yeah I am. I told her.

She and I both know it’s a lie.

She has seen my scars. The ones above my veins, the ones she Always misread as an attempt at life. I wish it was that simple. I never wanted to attempt my life. All I wanted was to see whether I would be able to feel something again. I’ve become numb to feelings. Paint me a hypocrite, but it’s true.

The only reason I’m thinking about leaving her or her leaving me is to see whether I’d left with those feelings people talk about. The pain of losing someone you loved. Knowing they’d be happy with someone else, I want to feel it.

It’s getting late. I know you’re not okay. She spoke to me with a concerned voice.

I know you’ve been through a lot, and you’re looking for something that’ll keep you engaged with your mind and emotions. It will come to you soon. So, until that, I’m here with you. Whenever you need, just talk to me. You know where to find me. I’m always here. She kept going on.

I know. Thank you for that. I told her.

As the sun takes its final dive into the ocean, I was looking at two things I cherish the most.

A beautiful sunset and a beautiful soul.

Maybe it isn’t that bad. Maybe I’ll be happy One day.

Photo by Samuel Theo Manat Silitonga on Pexels.com

I woke up to a text chime on my phone.

Wake up, sleepyhead. It said.

I didn’t sleep the last few days. It’s not because I couldn’t, but I forced myself to. I’m not a unique being who sees Night as a time to stay unconscious. I enjoy the silence of dawn and the cold atmosphere it has. It’s not like a sunset but to me, I find it easier to feel at dawn. Even though I feel nothing at all, I can try without being distracted by sounds and other things that haunt me.

Hey, are you awake? I know you are. Reply me. The phone chimes again.

Yeah, I am. What’s up? Anything wrong? I asked out of habit.

Why do I assume that everyone is in some distress when they reach me? Maybe it’s because I think whether people genuinely need to message me. I’ve never had someone genuinely asked me how am I doing. Whenever I get a message, it’s always followed up with a request for help or something that doesn’t concern me.

That’s before I found her. She’s always going on about her or just randomly asks about me. It’s refreshing because she never asks or requests as other people do.

What? Do you think I’m like those people who think that something’s wrong with them all the time? You’re weird. She replied.

Yeah, yeah, I know. I’m sorry. I replied out of habit.

What are YOU sorry for? She replied.

She’s right. I shouldn’t be sorry for someone else, but when I feel something about her, it’s much more different from others. It feels so close to my heart, almost as if I’m feeling it about myself. If I were to say that love synchronizes the hearts of people, would you call me a liar? Probably. But that’s how I think it is. Sometimes I just can’t explain it.

A love that is not love.

A relationship that isn’t a relationship.

We both exist in it but at the same time, we don’t. That’s what intrigued me about her in the first place.

Photo by Thu00e1i Huu1ef3nh on Pexels.com

Hey, can I ask you something? She questioned.

Ask away. I replied.

When will this end? She asked.

When will what end? I was confused.

This. You, Me, and everything in between. She replied.

What!? What are you talking about? I kept asking

Never mind, it’s getting late. I told you not to sleep in the afternoon because I always have to wake you up for the evening. She went on.

Will you please get up and come to see me? She asked.

Although I didn’t understand the latter part of the conversation, I said yes. I wanted to see the sunset. I’ve been visiting outside and observing it more frequently than before.

Sunsets are mysterious sometimes. That’s one of the reasons I love it this much.

Sitting on the bench, looking at how the sun trying to keep itself above the horizon, made me think It’s almost as if it doesn’t want to go. But I wanted it to go and cover the world with darkness.

Sometimes I don’t understand how she is okay with my trances or how I zone out in the middle of conversations. Maybe that’s why I find her alluring. How she can tolerate my weird habits. It’s quite weird, isn’t it? How love can make us do things that we never thought we would.

Work for the extra mile, chase a dream that you never thought you had, do things that you never thought you wanted. You keep discovering these things in your life when you are with someone, and sometimes it’s rewarding.

As I looked up from my trance, the sun has given up its battle with the horizon. It was drowning in the sea, and it’s probably the most beautiful sunset I have seen so far. I felt something special about it. Just as I feel about this day.

Until tomorrow then? She said, snapping me out of my concentration towards the sun.

No. I guess it’s about time, isn’t it? I told her with a faint voice.

Oh, have you finally decided what to do with me? She asked with her angelic voice which I grew attached to.

Yes, It’s about time I let you go, isn’t it? It’s not working well for me. All these years clinging on to something that doesn’t even exist anymore. I was at the end of my sanity while I muttered those words.

But what about the sunsets we shared? Are you gonna stop it too? She asked.

No, I don’t think I will. I still love the sunset and I will keep watching them, feeling them, but here on out it won’t be with you.

I just wanted to say thank you. For all the memories in the past few days. Although it’s just me, it almost felt as if it’s someone else.

As I reach the medications I skipped a couple of days ago, I wanted to make sure that I’ll be able to survive this on my own.

Do you think I will be able to do that? I asked her.

I think so. You don’t have to doubt it. There are no doubts in your mind. You have to believe me because I am you. She assured.

Okay then, I guess this is goodbye. It’s kinda weird saying goodbye to yourself.

As I restarted my medications, I can feel her voice fading away and cease out of existence. I was on my own after a while.

I walked out to the beach, where I have always found my happy place. The sunset, the beach, and everything in between.

~ Fin ~

item.title: A little confession
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I’m writing this in case if things go south.

I was not a firm believer of spontaneous vibing where you meet a random person out of nowhere and you feel like you’ve known them for sometime. To me that’s what you are, someone who appeared out of nowhere with absolutely no fucking reason and it made sense.

I instantly forgot what happened last time when i forced this vibe thing to happen and I despised it although it wasn’t much of a nightmare. Like it or not it was not the kind of relationship that I wanted to be in and it definitely was not the relationship i have imagined.

Tbat being said, I have no idea who you really are or what you truly bring to the table. All i know is being where i am right now, i shouldn’t be leading you on to a disappointment. At this age, Love or a relationship is not something you can experiment with. So I’ve decided to do the excruciatingly painful thing of looking at your life through your moments. It’s what you call self torture and I’m so used to it.

This seems to be a pointless blabbering by another hopeless romantic, it needed to be put out there, because i don’t want anyone to be concerned about it. I’m perfectly calm and accepting of current decisions and the outcomes of it. You should be too.

Peace.

item.title: The Grind
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Dying is easy.

Contrary to what people say it’s not a hard decision to take with all the shit that’s going around. You can simply say that you had enough or you’re worthless and you’re left with no choice but end it all.

Do you know what’s hard though? Living. No matter whatever the shit you have try living for a change. It’s hard and you always want to give up but it’s worth everything. When you achieve things in life you’ll realise that you never would’ve felt it if you decide to end your life. That’s what life is a well rewarding grind that you got to endure. Not everyone will go through it but to those who do you’re awesome and keep doing whatever you’re doing to make it happen.

item.title: The girl on the train
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
No, it’s not a story about a childhood friend. I was a young boy when i first saw her. I was on the way to school on a train and with the puberty and the hormones world was a different place than before. and then there she was, the woman i felt attracted to. I don’t want to label it as love but there was something about her that’s compelling and it drew me to her. The exhausting journey on the train turned out to be an hour in heaven to me. My attendance went up at school and i just loved every moment i spent looking at her.

I didn’t know who she was, where she is from and where she’s going at the time. Just the fact of her existence in that hour i spent on train was everything to me. It was the most innocent a love can be. I was wearing my blue shorts and white shirt to the school and i see her wearing a saree that made her look stunning. It was pure bliss to me. For years she never even notice that i was there. She couldn’t see how starstruck i was everyday looking at her. She was in living in the real world but to me, in that instance, she was my entire world.

They say that when you grow up the love grows up with you as well. I’ve experienced it first hand with her. It was quite simple actually. A boy, a girl and a heavy load of unrequited feelings because of how afraid i was to lose this feeling. It bothered me since I’ve seen her. What if i confessed and it goes south, will i be able look at her again? What if she’s married or have feelings for someone else? What if she’s not interested? I was afraid of going back to what it was before her. The love that was unspoken kept me from boring to death on the journey and i wanted it to last, so i stayed in silence. She was one person in the world but for that moment, that moment only she was my whole world.

I was no longer a student. While i grew up everything around me changed as well. The feelings, emotions, people and everything that made me who i am. But the only thing that didn’t change was the unrequited love i had for someone i barely knew. Someone that probably saw me growing up with time, someone who probably saw my eyes watching her but definitely someone who never knew that i had feelings for her. To this day nothing has changed in life on that journey. I’ll see her often on my way to work. I’ll imagine countless ways to talk to her, I’ll dream of wild scenarios that may happen and then I’ll get off at my stop like nothing happened. There’s a saying that first love never leaves your heart and i never thought it’d be unrequited.

As long as both she and I exist in the train journey this love will stay and maybe, just maybe if i find courage to defy my bubble of dreams I may talk with her but I’m addicted to the love that’s unspoken so I’ll most likely stay in silence and cherish the memories of our journey together as separate beings.

The love that’s unspoken is the love that’s most cherished because of all the possibilities that can happen.

item.title: I feel nothing
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I can write thousands of stories filled with cheap romance and yet feel nothing of it. The words i write seems fake to me but i find people making sense of it. In reality it’s just a desperate attempt to create a feeling that I have lost throughout the time. It used to be so genuine and calming but now it feels forced. Sometimes i just want to believe that it is possible to lose feelings and be apathetic, i really do. But why does it feel like my heart breaks every time when something goes bad.

It makes no fucking sense to both feel and not feel the same feeling in same context every time. Why the fuck would i feel sad and happy at the same time about myself reverting back to being who i was few years back. It shouldn’t be like that, I don’t want it to be like that. Although i like the emotional detachment in it, it hurts my mind alot.

I just wish i could feel nothing especially empathy like a sociopath. That way I might actually be free from this burden of useless feelings i have and finally have transcendence.

teddy

item.title: Alone and Depressed
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I just want to talk to someone,
Or scream how much of a pain i am in.
Even though I try to convince myself that this’ll end
It won’t.
It’s not Physical pain to be treated.
Everytime i see myself in mirror,
I see someone who’s struggling to survive.
I see someone who’s in pain,
I see someone who’s about to end it all.
I wish i had the resolve to go through it,
But i don’t.
I’m in an dilemma,
Should i stay alive and suffer so it might get better
or
Should I just end it all and let myself be free
It’s hard when you have to build from scratch
It’s hard when you have to put up the bread for family
It’s hard to live with delusional parents who thinks that we’re fine
The Truth is we’re not.
They fucked up,
They fucked it up all.
and they are too delusional to admit it.
I just want to leave and start over,
So at least I’ll be able to survive this mess.
I know this doesn’t make sense
But,
I hope you’d understand it.

Note to self - If you ever survive this, thank you for not giving up.

item.title: Writing, Depression and Curses
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It’s hard to describe a feeling when you’re not feeling it yourself. You can try, but it won’t work. It’ll end up being a version of your most common feeling. You can convince yourself that it is the real feeling but you know that it isn’t. Be it anything good or bad if you can’t feel it for yourself, you can’t.

I write depressing stuff. Mostly because when i got into writing, I was depressed most of the time. So every word I wrote was embedded with it, molded by it and came out as it is. I couldn’t write something Happy even if you held me at a gunpoint. Even now I’m less depressed or used to it now, i still can’t find my way around words. I guess I’m cursed for life and won’t be able to write something Happy after all. But if i write that’s all that matters to me. Writing ease the emotional distress I’m in. It saved me from lots of things and i owe my life to my hobby.

item.title: Dear 17 year old me
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Dear 17 year old me,

I’m sorry but i couldn’t become what you wanted.
I’m not a Singer or a Musician,
You wanted me to Marry by 25,
Well, fuck! It takes a lot to even find someone to marry,
I couldn’t find her, yet.
You were happy, I’m not
You weren’t depressed, I am
You had someone you thought you loved, I don’t.
You were wrong about society of future,
You were wrong about people around you,,
The ones who you thought would stay, they left.
The ones you thought who wouldn’t, they stayed.
You weren’t ready for all the heartbreaks,
You weren’t ready for all the sadness,
You weren’t ready for anything at all,
I don’t blame you,
You tried your best,

But,

Dear 17 year old me, it wasn’t enough.
People used you,
People threw you away,
You changed so you’d be safe,
If you could see what I’ve become,
You’d actually go through that one time.
I’m glad you didn’t,
I’m glad that I’m still here,
The life is still young,
Let’s make the most of it.

Your’s sincerely,
A much older You.

item.title: Spectator
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Someone asked me, what was the biggest sacrifice you’ve made for love. I had to think for awhile. There were many sacrifices i made in the name of love. Although I never managed to love down even one of those sacrifices, at the end of the day there was only one that stood out.

It was to see that my unrequited love being loved by someone else and me being happy for them. I was truly happy for her. She was living the dream. Someone who loves and cares for her, someone who always stood by her side. She never would’ve done half the things she did if i were to intervene. Therefore, the decision i took, the sacrifice i made even though no one really knew, was the biggest i did. And it was pretty much the only sacrifice i can give a worth to.

item.title: Me, but not me.
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I would often wonder how i wrote whatever i wrote back then. Whenever i look at them or read them i don’t see myself writing them at all. It’s like i was possessed by some entity before I wrote what i wrote. Every word and sentence is pure act of someone else but me. It gets a little weird when those writings begin to relate to what i am in today. It’s like i knew where i would be and wanted to take a shot at me when time is ripe. Everytime i read my words, i see someone else other than me.

item.title: Why do you keep coming back to me?
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
“I Loved you.” She insisted.

See, That’s where you’re wrong. you never loved me. you thought you did, but you didn’t. you were looking for validation. at that moment of your life you needed someone weaker or unfortunate than you. you wanted someone to suffer more than you. i was the scapegoat in your quest to find such a victim.

whenever you feel down or insecure you would ask me how my day was, Because you knew my day would’ve been shittier than yours was. it always was.

and then with the time i was moving up through life, i was getting in to good communities, good job, friends that you couldn’t tolerate, i was moving away from your criteria. then your fits, tantrums grew to the point that i had to leave you.

and I did.

So, let me answer my question to you. “Why do you keep coming back to me?”

Because, you still keep seeking that validation from someone. but this time, this time i know better. i am better.

So, sorry that i loved you, even if you didn’t.

item.title: The girl from the web..
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I wouldn’t exactly call her a ball of pure happiness. she was grey, her heart was pure, but her life filled with darkness. she was someone you would call a flight risk. yet, i found her quite compelling. a puzzle to solve. the way i met her, the way i kept talking with her, i have grown quite fond of her.

She was a paradox, scary yet intriguing.

the day i solve that puzzle, i might able to reach her. hopefully, someday.

item.title: Repetitive Motions
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I wonder why she even bother to come back in to my life again. i was not her ideal guy, i was never. but then again somehow she’s trying to get back to me. i’m not that special. only specialty i ever had was being an ultimate saboteur of relationships. i was a genius at it. and beause of that sole purpose i gave up relationships. not because i suffer or they suffer, because eventually it got boring. people became predictable and life was too clichéd. Somehow i began to see things repeating itself. it’s like my life ran out of things to do. so now it’s trying to bring back blasts from past. so i am reliving things i have experienced before and it’s so boring. Just like how that girl who went away with another guy. And she asked me why did i never asked her out. I was at loss of words. What should I say? I have nothing to say at all. I screwed it up, i screwed it up big time. And i have no excuses.

item.title: Hopes
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Tick tock, Tick tock time’s running out. Every breath i take seems like it’s the last one. I was wondering what I’m gonna do with my life like always. It seems that things won’t go in my way, never. Life was never is, but I wasn’t expecting it to be this hard. If it was just a bit easier so i can sinply manage.

item.title: Writers Block
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Writers block is the worst thing that can happen to a writer. It keeps happening to me. No matter how much I try to keep up with it all fails. I suddenly lose motivation, my writings get deleted because i forgot to save it or many others reasons that are completely out of ordinary. Still i try because once in a blue moon i write something mind blowing that makes me flip out and ask myself did i do that? So here I am for the nth time and hoping this would stick and hoping I can pull out some rabbits out of hat or at least a carrot. Before I go,

A person who loves you going to keep you to themselves that instead of asking the world from you, world ask you back from them.

item.title: Note to myself..
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I used to be a good writer. But something stopped me from writing. I never wanted to take a pen or open up a blog draft again. I was consumed to a point where i despised reading. I despised every aspect of it. It’s like breaking up with your long-term partner and keep saying you’d do fine without her. Eventually you’ll learn how to do it but you won’t ever be the same again. I wrote this little note as a reminder to myself. So if you’re reading this teddy, don’t give up on writing. That’s where your heart lies.

item.title: Returning to Blogging
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Okay, This Sucks. I’m known to rant about everything but in reality I’m doing it all in my head. No real words, No Audience. I rant to make myself comfortable or to escape from Life itself. I used to be this bright kid who spew sunshine out of my ass or i think i was. I can’t exactly remember as depression sweep me of my feet and made me its bitch. So I decided to rant on a blog which i created 4 years ago, and have 2 posts. that’s how lazy i was. Very.

item.title: Guess What!
item.author: Teddy
item.content: The mere illusion of everlasting love may console you for a while but the life can not built upon that. more you depend from the others you’ll get sucked in to darkness known as empathy. to build your life taste the pain of failures, the hardship of search and the learn to take risks. and most importantly learn the fact that nobody truly cares. This is a battlefield for you to survive. Cheers!
